title: Internal Injuries
metaTitle: Internal Injuries from Accidents — Symptoms & Legal Rights
metaDescription: Internal injuries from accidents are life-threatening and often not immediately apparent. Learn symptoms, emergency treatment, and your rights.
description: Internal injuries — including organ damage, internal bleeding, and thoracic and abdominal trauma — are among the most dangerous accident injuries because they are not always visible or immediately apparent. Delayed diagnosis can be life-threatening, making prompt medical evaluation critical after any high-impact accident.
symptoms: Shoulder pain (referred pain from diaphragm irritation — can signal internal bleeding)
Rapid heart rate and low blood pressure (signs of hemorrhagic shock)
Blood in urine, vomit, or stool
Deep bruising of the abdomen or chest without obvious external cause
Chest pain or difficulty breathing (pneumothorax, hemothorax)
Swelling or firmness of the abdomen
longTermEffects: Organ damage requiring surgical repair or removal (splenectomy, bowel resection)
Increased infection risk after organ damage or surgery
Diabetes or hormonal imbalances following pancreatic trauma
Chronic kidney dysfunction following renal injury
Psychological trauma from life-threatening injury and hospitalization
Reduced quality of life due to dietary restrictions or ongoing medical management
treatmentOptions: Emergency stabilization and fluid resuscitation for hemorrhagic shock
CT scan, ultrasound (FAST exam), or diagnostic peritoneal lavage for assessment
Emergency exploratory laparotomy or thoracotomy for severe injuries
Angioembolization to control arterial bleeding non-surgically
Intensive care unit (ICU) monitoring during critical phase
Non-operative management with careful monitoring for select solid organ injuries
Long-term follow-up with specialist surgeons and physicians
commonCauses: High-speed car, truck, and motorcycle accidents
Seatbelt compression injuries in severe crashes
Blunt force trauma from defective products or equipment
